name: Mixtral 8x7B
brainrotOneliner: 8 experts, 2 active at a time. 46B parameters but feels like 13B at inference. the MoE concept proof that changed everything.
keyInnovations: Sparse Mixture of Experts — 8 feed-forward expert blocks, only 2 active per token
Sliding window attention enables efficient 32k context handling
Outperforms LLaMA 2 70B while activating only a fraction of the parameters
First major open-source MoE model — proved the architecture is accessible, not just Google-scale
brainrotSummary: Mixtral is the model that proved MoE isn't just for trillion-parameter closed labs. 8 experts, route to 2 at a time, watch your active parameter count drop to ~13B while maintaining 46B worth of knowledge. it beats LLaMA 2 70B on most benchmarks at effectively half the inference cost. the efficiency speedrun that made everyone take Mistral seriously.

comparisons.gpt2: GPT-2 never heard of experts. Mixtral said sparsity is the future.
comparisons.gpt3: GPT-3 is dense and large. Mixtral is sparse and gets similar results for less compute.
comparisons.llama2: Mixtral beats LLaMA 2 70B while being cheaper to run. efficiency wins.
comparisons.gpt4: Mixtral is trying to close the gap on GPT-4 without the budget. getting closer.
comparisons.claude3: Mixtral is Apache 2.0 and open. Claude 3 is proprietary and constitutional. vibes differ.
comparisons.llama3-instruct: Mixtral MoE vs LLaMA 3 dense. both excellent open models, different architectural bets.
comparisons.deepseek-v3: Mixtral proved the MoE concept. DeepSeek V3 took it to 671B and made it even more efficient.
comparisons.grok-1: Mixtral is clean and efficient. Grok-1 is massive and chaotic. different MoE energy.
